Dr. Yana Ning

M.D. Xuanwu Medical School, Beijing, China, 1979.

M.S. Cytogenetics, Capital Institute of Medicine, Beijing, China, 1987.

Thesis: 1. A Short Term Safety Study of Transcervecal Chorionic Villi Samples during Early Pregnancy,

Thesis 2. Study on Common Fragile Sites in Chorionic Villi Cells Cultured in vitro.

1979-1988 Resident Physician, Dept. of Obstetrics and Gynecology in Beijing.

Post doctoral experience in human genome mapping at the Salk Institute and in signal transduction at Washington University Medical Center, St. Louis.

Joined the Institute in 1996. Currently working on gene evolution of vertebrate genes, including oxytocin and vasopressin through Fugu, other fish, and the acorn worm.
